payload.source_text:
Project Kickoff & Sync Notes - SmartHome Hub v2 (May 2026)

Talked with Janez from the hardware engineering team. He is worried about the new 
Chalcogenide optics availability. If the supplier delays the lens delivery by more 
than two weeks, we will completely miss our initial prototype assembly deadline, 
which is a massive roadblock. We need to find an alternative supplier by next Friday 
just in case.

Anyway, for the software side, the core priority right now is that the dev team must 
implement the Model Context Protocol (MCP) gateway for agent-to-agent communication. 
Marko should start working on the Rust module topology layout immediately so we know 
how the agents will talk to each other. He thinks he can wrap up the initial design by 
June 15th, which would be awesome because that's our internal architecture freeze milestone.

Oh, also, Maja from marketing reminded us that we must comply with the new EU data 
privacy guidelines for IoT devices before we launch any public beta. If we fail the 
compliance audit, we could face severe fines. Someone needs to draft a data compliance 
checklist before the end of the month. 

Let's make sure we schedule a follow-up review with all major stakeholders (Janez, 
Marko, Maja, and the steering committee) once the Rust architecture is finalized. 
I'll handle setting up the calendar invites.

Meeting notes from Monday standup:
Alice will finish the API integration by this Friday.
John is the project sponsor and needs weekly status updates.
Risk: the third-party payment gateway may not be ready before our Q3 launch milestone.
Current issue: CI pipeline is broken and blocking all deployments.
Target: go-live by September 30th.

finish the API integration

payload.source_text:
Sprint review today. Action items:
- Fix the critical login bug affecting all mobile users — urgent, in progress since yesterday
- Release v2.3 by end of Q2 — already slipped once
- Sarah (product owner) confirmed scope freeze
- Risk: third-party payment API is unstable, could block release
- Vendor contract renewal overdue — legal flagged as closed last week

payload.source_text:
Sprint planning for Q3 — 2026-05-28

We agreed to deliver the Logseq export v2 by June 15. Alice is the lead on this.
There's a risk that the Logseq API changes could break the current export format before we finish.
The CI pipeline needs to be updated to run the full test suite on every PR — Bob will handle this by end of next week.
Open issue: the multi-project registry occasionally returns stale data when two projects share the same name prefix.

The CI pipeline needs to be updated to run the full test suite on every PR

payload.source_text:
Standup 2026-05-28

Alice: finished ontology_suggest Stage 9. Will start on Logseq export v2 today.
Bob: blocked on CI config — waiting for DevOps to grant pipeline permissions. Escalation needed.
Carol: reviewed the multi-project registry stale data issue. Root cause identified — missing cache invalidation on rename. Fix is low complexity, will submit PR today.

proposals.rationale: Milestones typically start in a 'pending' state.
Migration to a new auth service is a critical project component.
Milestones typically start in a 'pending' state.
A major release milestone is typically of high priority.
Stakeholders are generally 'active' unless specified otherwise.
Milestones typically start in a 'pending' state.
An internal beta is an important step towards a full release.
Risks are 'open' until mitigated, accepted, or closed.
A risk that 'could block release' should be elevated to high priority.
A 'critical login bug affecting all mobile users' warrants a high priority.
Issues are 'open' until they are in progress, resolved, or closed.
An 'overdue' vendor contract renewal is a significant issue.
Even achieved milestones can have a priority for historical context.
Tasks typically start in a 'todo' state.
Writing a 'migration runbook before cutover' is a critical preparatory task.
Stakeholders are generally 'active' unless specified otherwise.
Risks are 'open' until mitigated, accepted, or closed.
Unhandled 'token expiry edge cases' can lead to significant issues, warranting a medium priority.
Stakeholders are generally 'active' unless specified otherwise.
The task to 'write migration runbook before cutover' is a prerequisite for the 'migration to new auth service' milestone.
The 'Deploy the payment gateway' milestone is likely related to the overall 'Release v2.3' given their shared Q2 timeframe.
The risk of an 'unstable third-party payment API' directly 'could block release' v2.3.
A 'critical login bug' affects the product owner (Sarah) who is responsible for the product.
The 'Vendor contract renewal overdue' issue needs to be assigned to a stakeholder for resolution.
An 'overdue vendor contract renewal' is a significant issue that should be escalated to the product owner (Sarah).
The task to 'write migration runbook' needs to be assigned to a stakeholder.
The risk of 'token expiry edge cases not yet handled' directly affects the 'migration to new auth service' milestone.
Unhandled 'token expiry edge cases' in an auth migration are significant and should be escalated to the product owner (Sarah).
